title: rubus crispus
intro: Rubus crispus, also known as woodland bramble or craggy blackberry, is a species of blackberry native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of Rubus crispus include its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming Rubus crispus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. Rubus crispus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and jams, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
